title: Indulgent Iced Filipino Potatoes Ginataang Bilo Bilo
description: A creamy, floral Filipino dish featuring charlotte potatoes, miso-marinate, and a Mediterranean twist. Perfect for warm weather.
instructions: Peel and dice the Charlotte potatoes into bite-sized pieces.
In a blender or food processor, combine the miso paste, ginger, garlic, and 1 tablespoon of sugar to make a marinade.
Place the diced potatoes in a large bowl and pour the marinade over them. Mix until the potatoes are evenly coated.
Let the potatoes marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Heat the coconut milk in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the marinated potatoes, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Add the macapuno strings to the saucepan and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until they are heated through.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
Cover the saucepan with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until chilled.
Serve the ginataang bilo bilo cold, garnished with toasted coconut flakes if desired.
Garnish with edible flowers, if desired, for a more indulgent look.
